name: Ubuntu
advantages: Everything in the ADL guides is tested on it
knownIssues: snapd does not work under proot — use apt or standalone builds
summary: The default and recommended ADL distribution.

name: Debian
advantages: Same apt workflow as the guides
knownIssues: Backports may be needed for newer apps
summary: A drop-in alternative if you prefer Debian stable.

name: Arch Linux
performance: Excellent when tuned; rolling updates need attention
disadvantages: Rolling release can break under proot
knownIssues: Keyring initialization sometimes needed after install (pacman-key --init)
summary: For experienced users who want current software.

name: Fedora
knownIssues: Some SELinux-adjacent tooling misbehaves under proot
summary: Solid, but fewer ADL-specific footsteps to follow.

name: Alpine
disadvantages: musl libc breaks some prebuilt binaries
knownIssues: glibc-only apps (e.g. VS Code) need workarounds or won't run
summary: Minimalist choice for constrained devices and terminal-first use.
